Eco-friendly house music festival — house music in nature. May annually. 30+ friendly, kid/pet/vegan/foreigner-friendly. Sauna bus, morning yoga, live bands, disco aerobics, crafting, karaoke, camping, hammocks. @the_airhouse
Specifically recommended for foreigners with tattoos — 100% stress-free. Paradise City complex near Incheon Airport. Great for a post-flight or pre-flight soak.
Outdoor charcoal sauna near Sinchon. Search 찜질방 in Naver Maps for nearest location.
Luxury jjimjilbang experience. Slightly outside central Seoul but worth the trip.
Trendy affordable Korean accessories — folding sunglasses, bags, rain boots, seasonal accessories. 12 Myeong-dong 8-na-gil Jung-gu Seoul. Open daily 10:30–23:00. naver.me/xGFxCi0c
American-style cozy bistro from Samcheong-dong now in Gwanghwamun. Creative dishes you can only find in Korea. naver.me/Goivggyk
Korea's largest bookstore. Not just books — K-stationery, stickers, music, unique Korean souvenirs. Easy to lose track of time here. naver.me/xtgpqcS3
Multi-story accessory heaven. Thousands of earrings, necklaces, bags, hats at very affordable prices. Open late. Perfect last stop of the day. naver.me/Gubf9M30
Private pods with curtains, blankets, pillows, leg massagers. Order ramyeon/tteokbokki to your pod. ~₩3,000–4,000/hr. Chains: Nolsoop (놀숲) and Beoltoon (벌툰). Package deal = 1hr + drink best value.

At Myeongdong Station Exit 2. English/Japanese/Chinese/Spanish speaking doctor. Cupping therapy, acupuncture, circulation medicine, wild ginseng injection, chuna therapy, facial lifting, weight loss. Check-up ₩132,000. Healing course C ₩242,000. @yonghadaclinic_en — DM for prices. naver.me/FORmG2el
Celebrity-level hair stylist @rollingjay_siwon__ (women) & @rollingjay_siwon__men. Teaches styling at home. Book via DM, Kakao ID: ruddo462, or Naver. naver.me/F9N4yI6L
Get new glasses in 10 minutes. No reservation or prescription needed. Much cheaper than home. Any optical shop works — look for them near major streets.
Must-buy: Sesame oil (참기름), rice syrup (조청), plum extract (매실청), coin broth (코인육수). Available at E-mart / Lotte Mart / Homeplus. Premium versions at department stores and traditional markets.
Curated Naver Map of ALL Mija's Seoul recommendations — restaurants, spas, shops. Direct link: naver.me/FNtroVSQ
First thing she did arriving in Korea — rejuran treatment. Foreign-friendly. Also has Myeongdong (@vands_myeongdong) and Gangnam branches. One of the most recommended foreigner-friendly clinics.
Foreign-friendly injectable clinic (filler, botox etc). 30s & wedding era crowd. @vands_myeongdong | @vands_myeongdong_gangnam_eng for Gangnam branch.
Tune Liner treatment — jawline contouring. No bruising, instant results. Alternative to inmode/Shrunik. @dreraserclinic. Also does laser/inmode. Sinsa-dong Gangnam.
FREE comprehensive skin analysis before Olive Young shopping. Tells skin age + type + 6 characteristics + customised routine. Only recommends what you actually need. @inkoseoul_official
Favourite Korean pharmacy in Gangnam — curated skincare edit. Right near the beauty clinics. @jeilgrand_pharmacy_global
In-store free skin analysis with consultant. Takes face measurements, explains skin type + 6 key characteristics, customises a routine. No upselling.
Viral spicy chicken feet restaurant. Wait ~1hr but grab a ticket and roam nearby then come back. 📍3 Wangsimni-ro 22-gil, Doseon-dong, Seongdong-gu, Seoul
Concept café where it rains 24/7 inside — stay cosy with coffee + croissant even in 30°C heat. @rain.report

Place Name: Korea Bakery World Cup Winner's Bakery
Description / Notes: Bakery run by team leader of Korea's Bakery World Cup winning team. Incredible textures and flavours. Worth a day trip from Seoul.

Roman&You makeup brand's pop-up Pink Office in Seongsu. @romandyou — worth a visit if the pop-up is running.
Cat café in Hongdae. Admission includes unlimited self-serve tea and coffee. Snacks available to feed cats. @roofcatme_hongdae
Baseball game in Seoul = full-on party atmosphere, not just sport. Beer, fried chicken, cheering culture. DM @hungrypursuit 'SEOUL BASEBALL' for booking guide.
World's first AI-developed ice cream shop + research lab. Exclusive flavours: wasabi, Cheetos, truffle. 📍201 Nonhyeon-ro, Gangnam District, Seoul. @baskinrobbinskorea
Very local park — foreigners rarely visit. Beautiful walk OR intense hike on different paths. Great for experiencing authentic Busan neighbourhood life.
Multi-attraction + transit pass. Makes navigating Seoul significantly easier. Use code KAY-SEOULPASS for ₩5,000 discount on website/app.
5-star hotel in Myeongdong. Great for first-timers. Detailed foreigner-friendly review available. @lottehotelseoul
Luxury hotel on Jeju Island. @JWmarriotjeju — worth it for a Jeju splurge.
Traditional Korean medicine appointment — cupping, herbal, posture, acupuncture. Under $45/appointment. Highly recommended for visitors.
Hair clinic/salon. Reportedly even cheaper than Turkey for hair treatments. @hairofseoul — DM/Kakao/Instagram for bookings.
15-step Korean hair spa. Stunning studio, friendly staff. @parkjun2342 for booking.
Granhand Marine Orchid — specifically called out as a standout fragrance in the lineup. @granhand_official

@yonseian.painclinic — ANS test, X-ray, 3D AI posture analysis, massage/physical therapy. Multiple saves pointing here. Specialists in back/neck pain. Reached out to creators for sponsored reviews but genuine results reported.
X-ray, body scan on treadmill, posture/balance analysis, back and neck treatment. Used by @reikim.artist who returns annually for back treatment post-childbirth.
90-min full-body stretch session — leaves you feeling completely reset. @stretch_u_care_global. 📍408 510 Dogok-ro Gangnam District Seoul.
Full body MRI scan. @medione_medical_global. Part of @celinelinarte's Seoul health clinic sweep.
Acne/breakout treatment clinic. @relieve_clinic_ — 3rd visit review = genuine use. Bundle treatment for active breakouts.

8-storey wholesale clothing building near DDP. Open until 5am. Mostly wholesale — vendors may ignore solo foreign shoppers. Better bets inside: Mimiline and NYUNYU, both open until 5am, foreigner-friendly, card accepted, tax-free.

BEST Korean street food location. Coin-based food system — exchange money for coins at entrance, fill a tray from multiple stalls, eat communally. Solo-traveller friendly. 📍Jongno-gu Seoul near Gyeongbokgung.
Packed with Koreans of all ages — zero tourist energy. Caption deliberately vague (doesn't even name it). DM @cutthelandline 'seoul eats' for exact address.
@dianainseoul recommends staying in Chungmuro — between Myeongdong and Dongdaemun on lines 3/4. Bustling but non-tourist. Good transit hub for both sides of the city.
Korean niche fragrance brand. Flagship carries Colline, Gureum, and Rondo24 — Rondo24 specifically called a repeat-buy. @bibiang_official.
Multi-storey Gentle Monster concept store. @gentlemonster — get the full art installation + eyewear experience.
Underground mall beneath Hongdae. Good for affordable fashion. Open late.
Not the typical tourist dept store. Curated local Korean brands. Worth the trip to Yeouido.
Korean fragrance + beauty brand flagship. 📍316-7 Seongsu-dong 2(i)-ga Seongdong-gu Seoul.
Korean niche fragrance. 📍737-1 Hannam-dong Yongsan-gu Seoul.
Korean niche fragrance boutique. 📍744-4 Hannam-dong Yongsan-gu Seoul.
Korean niche fragrance. 📍153-1 Changseong-dong Jongno-gu Seoul.
Korean niche fragrance brand in Seongsu. 📍16-33 Seongsu-dong 1(il)-ga Seongdong-gu Seoul.
@anj.mejia_ — jjimjilbang described as the most therapeutic thing. Modern version in Busan recommended over traditional. Search 찜질방 in Naver Maps when in Busan.
Bar/nightlife venue. @01compile — in @shoutoutluka's verified Seoul nightlife list.
Bar/nightlife venue. @zuhzuh.kr — in @shoutoutluka's verified Seoul nightlife list.
Bar in Hongdae. @lastchunsun_hongdae — in @shoutoutluka's verified Seoul nightlife list.
Bar/music venue. @ongroundmusicbar — in @shoutoutluka's verified Seoul nightlife list.
Men's hair salon. @highway_menshair — in @shoutoutluka's Seoul list.
Best sunset viewpoint in Seoul according to a Korean local. Hiking trail 40min for experienced, 60min beginner. 📍불암산 공영주차장 Deokneung-ro 94-gil 73. Transit: Sanggye Station Exit 1.

Highly recommended experience — tells you exact body/color type and what styles, collar shapes, suit jacket vents suit you. Results can change over time with complexion. Book via Korean clinics or stylists.
@haeinholi's guide to apps you actually need in Korea. Key tip: get an eSIM with a Korean phone number — needed for food delivery apps, restaurant waitlists, and booking confirmations.
@phatlifeproject's hack to clear Incheon immigration in 15 min instead of 1–1.5hrs.
Confirmed great stay. @weave_suites via @liveanywhere.korea @liveanywhere.me. Serviced apartment with Han River views.
'SO PEAK and cheap' for Incheon/Songdo visits. @central_park_hotel_songdo. Great if visiting Songdo as a day trip or arriving/departing from Incheon.
Multi-attraction pass. June promo code GENESIA-SEOULPASS for extra ₩5,000 off. Covers: Lotte World, N Seoul Tower, Nami Island, Gyeongbokgung + Hanbok, Seoul Aquarium, Realworld Seongsu.
Animal cafe where you can hold and interact with owls. Memorable, low-key experience in Akihabara.
Japanese outdoor/hiking brand - ultra-light jackets, travel clothes, high quality gear. Local favourite, often missed by tourists.
Shrine nestled in a lush forest, ~300 steps up to the Ugeto-iwa Cave at the top. Featured in the anime/film Hotarubi no Mori e. Go early on a weekday to avoid crowds, comfy shoes needed.
Tropical island getaway - beaches, snorkeling, island vibes. Very different pace from mainland Japan.
Iconic cherry blossom mountain in Nara, perfect day trip from Osaka/Kyoto (~2hr train + uphill bus). Walk down through layers of sakura - locals do the same walk. ~4hrs at a relaxed pace.
Quiet, emotional, local Japan - the lake that inspired Kimi no Na Wa (Your Name). Go up to Tateishi Park at sunset, bring a snack, and just watch.
One of the best onsen towns in Japan - mountains, cold air, hot water, tradition everywhere. Stay at least one night and try different baths.
Absolutely unreal gorge - turquoise water, dramatic cliffs, mythology. One of the standout Kyushu stops.
UNESCO village - straight out of a fairytale in winter. Snow-covered farmhouses, quiet nights. Stay overnight if possible; once day-trippers leave it's magic.
Some of the best nature in Japan - crystal rivers, alpine views, fresh mountain air. Go early and walk beyond the main bridge for quieter spots.
Old Japan at its best - historic post towns (Tsumago/Magome), forests, and history you can feel walking the old Nakasendo trail.
Custom pillow service by sleep experts - full posture/sleep assessment, test different heights/materials, pillow made on the spot. ¥33,000-44,000. Book a private consultation ahead.
Japanese outerwear engineered with decades of precision - puffer jackets and technical wear. 7 min walk from Meiji-Jingumae Station exit 7.
Down jacket specialist, located in the WAT building near Meiji-Jingumae Station exit 7 (~6 min walk). Pairs well with a Descente visit nearby.
Treasure trove for vintage Rolex and luxury watch hunting - huge selection, English/Mandarin-speaking staff. A must for watch collectors.
Animal cafe with otters - a fun, low-key activity to break up temple-hopping in Kyoto.
Watch wild snow monkeys soak in hot springs - iconic Nagano day trip.
Upscale fashion shopping street near Harajuku - flagship stores and coat/outerwear shopping.
Two weeks here barely scratches the surface - uncrowded alternative to the main Tokyo/Kyoto/Osaka route. Cherry blossoms with no crowds, great for autumn foliage too.
Early 5am wake-up hiking trail - worth it for the sunrise views over Alishan.

Japanese hotel chain right above Ximen Station (exit 2/3). Huge clean rooms, amenities bar in lobby, vacuum-wrap & luggage delivery service, water refill station each floor. Across from Ximending's attractions including The Red House.
"Little Switzerland" - try local Yunnan-inspired dishes, see sheep roaming green meadows with European-style farmhouses. Pairs well with Hehuanshan.
Breathtaking mountain views, standing above the clouds with alpine air. Hike side trails or drive to the top. Best for sunrise - consider staying overnight nearby and renting a car for flexibility.
Niche Asian perfumery - fragrances inspired by familiar flavours alongside scents you won't find elsewhere. Must-visit for niche perfume fans.

Misty forests, 1000+ year old cypress trees, the iconic Alishan Forest Railway, sea of clouds, and fairytale sunrise views. Entrance ~NT$300. Stay overnight to beat the crowds - HSR to Chiayi then bus/railway, or bus direct from Chiayi Station (~2.5hrs).
Stop on a 48hr Yilan itinerary - local farm experience.

Place Name: Su Ao Fish Port (蘇澳漁港)
Description / Notes: Fresh sashimi lunch straight off the boats at this fishing port.
Quick cliffside stop near Su'ao - distinctive tofu-block-shaped rock formations along the coast.
Black sand beach stop on the Yilan coastal route.
Popular night market to cap off a Yilan day trip.
Capital city and a great intro to Kyrgyzstan - visit the local museums and try beshbarmak (national dish) at Navat restaurant.

Best visited on a 3-4 day horseback trek - truly immersive nomadic experience, widely rated as a top excursion. Loop route via Kyzart: ride to Kyzart, then to the lake (cold dip + high-altitude volleyball, yurt with log burner), then back through stunning mountains. ★ top pick.
Starting point/staging village for Song-Kul horseback treks. Multi-course lunch with lots of tea at the stables on arrival.
Kyrgyzstan's largest lake - scenic landscapes, lots to see and do around the shore. Look out for eagle hunting demonstrations.
Hot springs valley near Karakol - a must-visit. Often combined with the Ala-Kul trek; many trek on foot from Karakol but it can also be done partly on horseback.
High-altitude turquoise lake - a top trekking destination near Karakol, frequently paired with Altyn Arashan in a multi-day route. ★ top pick.
Remote, treacherous journey but worth it - plan for 3 full days round trip. One of the most striking and least-visited alpine lakes in the country.
Walking tour of Gyeongdong market with local guide Wook Eun Shin. Authentic neighbourhood market, Korean BBQ, and real Seoul history. Much less touristy than Gwangjang.
Korean lymphatic drainage facial - they absolutely snatch you up here. High intensity treatment, results are immediate.
Fascinating non-skincare health assessment — full body wellness evaluation. Worth doing if interested in Korean medical tourism beyond aesthetics.
Best men's clothing spots in Seoul according to @rogerwh0 — includes Llege Official among others.
The real salt bread — buttery and crisp, nothing like the name implies. Jayeondo Sogeumppang (자연도소금빵) is the original. Will ruin you for other bread.
Top 9 treasures at the National Museum. Free entry, world class collection. Underrated by tourists who skip for palaces.
Magical afternoon with alpacas in Gangwon-do. Fluffy, gentle creatures in a gorgeous mountain setting. Perfect day trip escape from Seoul.
Perennial favourite — revisited twice a year. Known for kalguksu/mandu. Always worth the wait.
6 curated cafes worth visiting in Seoul — all vetted by a regular visitor.

Seoul's public bike system — download 'Seoul Bike' app, ~₩1,000 per rental, international cards OK, no sign-up needed. The 1-hour hack: ride for 1 hour straight, return within the hour, costs the flat rental rate only.
Key insight: plan day trips by distance and transit time or you'll waste hours. Framework for adding the right trips without over-scheduling.
Free premium hanbok rental (1.5hrs) at Neonadeuri Center — skip the expensive Bukchon rentals. Korean-only online booking but walk-in works. Hidden neighbourhood hanok village, far less crowded.
Easy paved trail to N Seoul Tower — bus stop takes you right to the start. Great for families, dates, solo runs. Go in the morning for fresh air.
Best tourism pass for Seoul — ₩5,000 off with code SYLVIA-SEOULPASS (valid to June 2026). Covers major attractions + transport.
Bars that play vinyl records or have DJs: Silly Aperitivo, Osem Seoul, Culsec Seoul, Nega Iso, Hills and Europa, FFF Samgakji.
The Hawaii of Korea — volcanic landscapes, citrus orchards, black sand beaches. Seongsan Ilchulbong (free sunrise), Hallim Park, O'Sulloc Tea Museum, Manjang Cave, Udo Island day trip.
Essential Jeju outdoor activity — volcanic hiking. Add this to any Jeju itinerary.
Better than Gwangjang Market according to locals — crispy fried peppers and fresh seafood you'll still be thinking about. @eunicexplores pick.
Challenging hike with stunning views over Seoul. Budget a full day. One of the best outdoor experiences within the city limits.
Korean-local pricing guaranteed. Free Anua gift set when booking ahead. 498 Gangnam-daero. 4M+ reviews via Unni app — the legit way to access Korean medical aesthetics at local rates.
Deep dive Jeju guide: stay in Aewol (west, sunset side) or Hallim/Hyeopjae (beach, quieter). Hike Hallasan, sea kayak Hamdeok, drive the Olle Trail.
Two trains from airport: Express (43 min, ₩11,000, Seoul Station only) vs All-Stop (66 min, ₩4,950, stops at Hongdae). Most solo travelers should take the All-Stop to Hongdae.
You will simply never catch them in Myeongdong again — skip it, spend that time elsewhere.
Oeadolgae Rock, Cheonjiyeon & Jeongbang Waterfalls in Seogwipo, Daepo Jusangjeolli Cliff, Camellia Hill Botanical Garden, Seongsan Ilchulbong sunrise peak.
Hidden coastlines, dreamy cafes, volcanic views. Designed around the parts of Jeju that feel unreal.
Hwangnyeongsan Beacon (황령산 봉수대) — grab an Uber most of the way up, then a short walk to the lookout. Book Uber 10-15 mins before you want to leave. Best single thing to do in Busan.
Gollyunsan Mountain hang gliding site. 86 Mt., Chilpo-ri, Heunghae-eup. Unique activity outside the Seoul/Busan circuit.
Personalised Seoul itinerary planning service — good option if you want a guide tailored to past trips rather than a generic list.
Apps that actually work for navigating Korea: covers transport, maps, translation tools.
Manual massage therapy + physical therapy for chronic back pain. @yonseian.painclinic reached out to creator @jiragn — placebo or not, she felt better. Worth checking out for injury rehab.
Creator @bridgetteugarte called this one of the best bites of her life. Exact location unclear from caption — search the creator's page for the pin.

Korea travel tips for non-K-pop fans: kitchenware market (unique pottery at wholesale), baby/kids fashion street from Hoehyeon Station Exit 6, fashion accessories wholesale. Most street shops in Korea source from here. @mijainseoul's guide.

Three neighbourhoods worth returning to: Hannam-dong (uptown Hannam = quieter, curated, wander-worthy), Seongsu-dong (go even if crowded — new places keep opening), and a third. @kaynamiee's personal list.
@karenyuu_ summarizes every major Seoul area in one sentence each. Good orientation resource for first-timers.
Practical Korea travel tips from @dianainseoul — covers Chungmuro and Gangnam Unni area.
